key_insight: Hierarchical granular metamaterials combine architected grains, packed-grain topology, and local defects/gradients to achieve both high energy absorption and low transmitted peak force at low density.
engineering_relevance: The design offers a programmable route to lightweight impact-mitigating materials with tunable deformation and transport pathways, extending beyond purely mechanical performance.
method: Lightweight grains with hollow elliptical inclusions
Crystal-inspired packing of grains into hierarchical tessellations
Functional gradients and defects to steer contact networks and deformation
evidence_signal: The paper combines in situ nanomechanical tests, nonlinear computational modeling, and multiscale impact experiments to show improved absorption per mass and reduced peak force across length scales and materials.
watch_item: Performance depends on careful control of grain architecture and packing topology, so manufacturing complexity and defect sensitivity may limit practical scaling.
